name: Compound Interest Calculator
description: See how your investment grows with compounding.
formulaDescription: Compound interest formula where P is principal, r is rate, n is frequency, t is time

example.explanation: ₹50,000 at 6% annual interest compounded monthly for 5 years grows to ₹67,449.05.

faqs.question: How does compounding work?
faqs.answer: Interest is added to the principal, and future interest is earned on the new total.

faqs.question: Is compounding annual?
faqs.answer: It depends; many products compound quarterly or monthly for faster growth.

faqs.question: Does higher frequency always give more returns?
faqs.answer: Yes, more frequent compounding results in slightly higher returns over time.
